title: NOC 14102 – Personnel Clerks | GTR Immigration
description: Personnel Clerks play a crucial role in supporting Canada’s workforce by handling recruitment records, training documentation, employee files, and HR-related coordination. Classified under NOC 14102, this occupation continues to see steady hiring across both private companies and government organizations, offering potential immigration pathways through Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), employer-supported work permits, and long-term PR strategies.

classification.teer: Ongoing hiring in HR and staffing divisionsEntry-level TEER category with advancement optionsEmployer-sponsored rolesStable clerical and administrative demand
classification.major_group: 141 – Office, court, and data support occupations
classification.minor_group: 1410 – Office support and court services occupations

common_job_titles: Classification clerk – human resources Employment clerk Human resources assistant Human resources clerk Labour relations clerk Personnel services clerk Staffing clerk Training clerk
duties: Process and register staffing, recruitment, training, and grievance records
Maintain manual and digital HR filing systems
Compile reports related to personnel activities
Respond to inquiries from employees and the public
Assist with job postings and applicant screening
Coordinate internal and external training programs
employment_requirements: College-level courses in personnel or human resources administration (often preferred)
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 3,100 new positions for Personnel Clerks, while 3,200 job seekers are expected—making employer sponsorship, regional selection, and professional immigration planning particularly important for overseas candidates.
additional_information: Personnel Clerks are commonly employed in:
Human resources departmentsGovernment agenciesEducational institutionsHealthcare organizationsCorporate offices
Annual earnings typically range between $34,600 and $65,127 CAD, depending on province and experience.
